steps.title: Map the two sentences
steps.content: Sentence 1: greater iconicity $\Rightarrow$ sign more likely to have been learned (iconicity helps acquisition). Sentence 2: the iconicity-acquisition correlation was lower than the frequency-acquisition correlation (iconicity matters less than another factor).

steps.title: Identify the relationship
steps.content: Sentence 2 concedes a limit on Sentence 1's finding: yes, iconicity helps, but a different factor mattered more. That is a concession transition.

steps.title: Eliminate per choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — In fact,: signals intensification of the prior point, but Sentence 2 weakens, not strengthens, the role of iconicity (logic violated). ✗
  • Choice B — In other words,: signals restatement, but Sentence 2 introduces a new factor (frequency) and a comparison, not a paraphrase (logic violated). ✗
  • Choice C — Granted,: concedes that iconicity's role, while real, is outranked by frequency — exactly the relationship between the two sentences. ✓
  • Choice D — As a result,: signals cause-and-effect, but Sentence 2 is not a consequence of Sentence 1; it is a contrast with another correlation (logic violated). ✗
That matches Choice C — Granted,.
